segments.text: Now King David was old and advanced in years; and they covered him with clothes, but he couldn’t keep warm.
Therefore his servants said to him, {
Let a young virgin be sought for my lord the king. Let her st and before the king, and cherish him; and let her lie in your bosom, that my lord the king may keep warm.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So they sought for a beautiful young lady throughout all the borders of Israel, and found Abishag the Shunammite, and brought her to the king.
The young lady was very beautiful; and she cherished the king, and served him; but the king didn’t know her intimately.
Then Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself, saying, {
I will be king.
segments.speaker: Adonijah

segments.text: Then he prepared him chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
His father had not displeased him at any time in saying, {
Why have you done so?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and he was also a very handsome man; and he was born after Absalom.
He conferred with Joab the son of Zeruiah and with Abiathar the priest; and they followed Adonijah and helped him.
But Zadok the priest, Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, Nathan the prophet, Shimei, Rei, and the mighty men who belonged to David, were not with Adonijah.
Adonijah killed sheep, cattle, and fatlings by the stone of Zoheleth, which is beside En Rogel; and he called all his brothers, the king’s sons, and all the men of Judah, the king’s servants;
but he didn’t call Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah, and the mighty men, and Solomon his brother.
Then Nathan spoke to Bathsheba the mother of Solomon, saying, {
Haven’t you heard that Adonijah the son of Haggith reigns, and David our lord doesn’t know it?
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: Now therefore come, please let me give you counsel, that you may save your own life and your son Solomon’s life.
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: Go in to King David, and tell him, ‘Didn’t you, my lord the king, swear to your servant, saying, “Assuredly Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: ? Why then does Adonijah reign?’
Behold, [^1] while you are still talking there with the king, I will also come in after you and confirm your words.”

footnotes.text: + {   "type": "speech",   "text": "Behold" } , from  {   "type": "speech",   "text": "הִנֵּה" }, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

segments.text: Bathsheba went in to the king in his room. The king was very old; and Abishag the Shunammite was serving the king.
Bathsheba bowed and showed respect to the king. The king said, {
What would you like?
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: She said to him, {
My lord, you swore by Yahweh[^2] your God[^3] to your servant, ‘Assuredly Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne.’
segments.speaker: The king

footnotes.text: + {   "type": "speech",   "text": "Yahweh" }  is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered  {   "type": "speech",   "text": "LORD" } (all caps) in other translations.

segments.text: Now, behold, Adonijah reigns; and you, my lord the king, don’t know it.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: He has slain cattle and fatlings and sheep in abundance, and has called all the sons of the king, Abiathar the priest, and Joab the captain of the army; but he hasn’t called Solomon your servant.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: You, my lord the king, the eyes of all Israel are on you, that you should tell them who will sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Otherwise it will happen, when my lord the king sleeps with his fathers, that I and my son Solomon will be considered criminals.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Behold, while she was still talking with the king, Nathan the prophet came in.
They told the king, saying, {
Behold, Nathan the prophet!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: When he had come in before the king, he bowed himself before the king with his face to the ground.
Nathan said, {
My lord, King, have you said, ‘Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne’?
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: For he has gone down today, and has slain cattle, fatlings, and sheep in abundance, and has called all the king’s sons, the captains of the army, and Abiathar the priest. Behold, they are eating and drinking before him, and saying, ‘Long live King Adonijah!’
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: But he hasn’t called me, even me your servant, Zadok the priest, Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and your servant Solomon.
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: Was this thing done by my lord the king, and you haven’t shown to your servants who should sit on the throne of my lord the king after him?
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: Then King David answered, {
Call Bathsheba in to me.
segments.speaker: King David

segments.text: She came into the king’s presence and stood before the king.
The king vowed and said, {
As Yahweh lives, who has redeemed my soul out of all adversity,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: most certainly as I swore to you by Yahweh, the God of Israel, saying, ‘Assuredly Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne in my place;’ I will most certainly do this today.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Then Bathsheba bowed with her face to the earth and showed respect to the king, and said, {
Let my lord King David live forever!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: King David said, {
Call to me Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada.
segments.speaker: King David

segments.text: They came before the king.
The king said to them, {
Take with you the servants of your lord, and cause Solomon my son to ride on my own mule, and bring him down to Gihon.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Let Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anoint him there king over Israel. Blow the trumpet, and say, ‘Long live King Solomon!’
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Then come up after him, and he shall come and sit on my throne; for he shall be king in my place. I have appointed him to be prince over Israel and over Judah.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada answered the king, and said, {
Amen. May Yahweh, the God of my lord the king, say so.
segments.speaker: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada

segments.text: As Yahweh has been with my lord the king, even so may he be with Solomon, and make his throne greater than the throne of my lord King David.
segments.speaker: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada

segments.text: So Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites went down and had Solomon ride on King David’s mule, and brought him to Gihon.
Zadok the priest took the horn of oil from the Tent, and anointed Solomon. They blew the trumpet; and all the people said, {
Long live King Solomon!
segments.speaker: all the people

segments.text: All the people came up after him, and the people piped with pipes, and rejoiced with great joy, so that the earth shook with their sound.
Adonijah and all the guests who were with him heard it as they had finished eating. When Joab heard the sound of the trumpet, he said, {
Why is this noise of the city being in an uproar?
segments.speaker: all the people

segments.text: While he yet spoke, behold, Jonathan the son of Abiathar the priest came; and Adonijah said, {
Come in; for you are a worthy man, and bring good news.
segments.speaker: all the people

segments.text: Jonathan answered Adonijah, {
Most certainly our lord King David has made Solomon king.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: The king has sent with him Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and they have caused him to ride on the king’s mule.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king in Gihon. They have come up from there rejoicing, so that the city rang again. This is the noise that you have heard.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: Also, Solomon sits on the throne of the kingdom.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: Moreover the king’s servants came to bless our lord King David, saying, ‘May your God make the name of Solomon better than your name, and make his throne greater than your throne;’ and the king bowed himself on the bed.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: Also thus said the king, ‘Blessed be Yahweh, the God of Israel, who has given one to sit on my throne today, my eyes even seeing it.’
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: All the guests of Adonijah were afraid, and rose up, and each man went his way.
Adonijah was afraid because of Solomon; and he arose, and went, and hung onto the horns of the altar.
Solomon was told, {
Behold, Adonijah fears King Solomon; for, behold, he is hanging onto the horns of the altar, saying, ‘Let King Solomon swear to me first that he will not kill his servant with the sword.’
segments.speaker: Solomon was

segments.text: Solomon said, {
If he shows himself a worthy man, not a hair of his shall fall to the earth; but if wickedness is found in him, he shall die.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: So King Solomon sent, and they brought him down from the altar. He came and bowed down to King Solomon; and Solomon said to him, {
Go to your house.
segments.speaker: Solomon
